name: Alewa Heights
desc: Quiet ridge neighborhood above Liliha with sweeping harbor views — established in the 1920s, local feel, winding roads.
insider: The views of Honolulu Harbor, downtown, and the ocean from Alewa Drive are spectacular — especially at night with the city lit up
Roads are narrow and winding with limited street parking — know your route before heading up and watch for blind curves
The neighborhood retains an old-Hawaii residential character most of metro Honolulu has lost — wooden homes, big yards, fruit trees
Alewa Heights is one of the cooler spots in town — the elevation brings genuine trade wind breezes on hot days
The neighborhood was established in the 1920s and retains many original plantation-style and early mid-century homes
No shops or restaurants up here — Liliha Bakery and the shops on Liliha Street below are your closest options
Some ridge-top homes get 270-degree views from Pearl Harbor to Diamond Head — at a fraction of Pacific Heights prices
Morning joggers and dog walkers own the quiet streets at dawn — one of the safest neighborhoods for walking in Honolulu
Bus service is limited up the hill — you really need a car for daily life
Many families have lived here for generations — the community is older, established, and genuinely neighborly
